title: General n-term (unweighted) AM-GM inequality
informal_statement: For a nonempty finite index set s and nonnegative reals z_i (i in s), the geometric mean (the product of the z_i raised to the power 1/|s|) is at most the arithmetic mean (the sum of the z_i divided by |s|).
informal_proof_idea: Instantiate Mathlib's weighted AM-GM (Real.geom_mean_le_arith_mean_weighted) with the constant weight 1/|s| on every index (nonnegative, and summing to 1 since |s| * (1/|s|) = 1 for nonempty s). The weighted conclusion's LHS product-of-powers collapses to (product)^(1/|s|) via Real.finsetProd_rpow, and its RHS sum-of-(weight*value) collapses to (1/|s|) * sum via Finset.mul_sum, matching the target's product/|s| division form.
formal_statement_pp: theorem general_amgm {ι : Type*} (s : Finset ι) (z : ι → ℝ) (hs : s.Nonempty) (hz : ∀ i ∈ s, 0 ≤ z i) : (∏ i ∈ s, z i) ^ ((s.card : ℝ))⁻¹ ≤ (∑ i ∈ s, z i) / s.card

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 71fafcd9), kernel_verified on the second attempt (step 1 kernel-failed with 'Unknown identifier geom_mean_le_arith_mean_weighted' -- the lemma lives under the Real namespace in this Mathlib rev; step 2 qualified it as Real.geom_mean_le_arith_mean_weighted and went through). Closes the 'General n-term AM-GM' backlog item in packets/elementary/inequalities/QUEUE.md, previously assumed to need new InequalityEstimateKit infrastructure ('defer until the 2/3/4-term ladder above is in place') -- turned out to be directly derivable from an existing Mathlib lemma (the weighted AM-GM with constant weights) rather than needing any new kit, once mathlib_search_declarations surfaced Real.geom_mean_le_arith_mean_weighted.
